Thu, Dec 4, 2025 · 6:45 PM to 9:30 PM CET

Utopicus Paseo de la Habana

Paseo de la Habana 9,11 (Entrada coworking Utopicus) · Madrid

Madrid dbt Meetup #7 (in-person)

Hosted by Shay B. and 2 others

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, DataOps, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍 Venue Host: Utopicus Habana (P.º de La Habana, 9, 11, 28036 Madrid)
🍕 Catering: Drinks & Pizza at the place of the event
🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

👉 To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

🗓️ Agenda:

18:45 - 19:00 | Welcome
19:00 - 19:05 | Introduction
19:05 - 19:15 | dbt Announcements
19:15 - 19:45 | Talk 1 - How to enable self-service analytics through the (dbt) semantic layer. (Astrafy)
19:45 - 20:15 | Talk 2 - Avoiding dbt mess. From 1000+ model dbt mono-repository to multi-project. (Mercadona Tech)
20:15 - 21h30 | Networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1:
In this session, you'll discover how semantic layers (dbt/Lightdashs) empowers all kind of users to navigate their data without technical knowledge.

Speaker bio:
Cris Navas is Data Engineer at Astrafy. With a strong background in IT and a passion for turning data into valuable insights, he loves the mix of technical problem-solving and creativity in data engineering.

---

🗣️Presentation #2:
In this session, we’ll take you behind the scenes of our journey to redefine data workflows and elevate data quality by breaking down a huge dbt repository into smaller dbt projects leveraging cookiecutter templates. Also, we enforced the software engineering best practices to keep the highest data-quality standards, enabling our users to do TDD and defining data-contracts between the different data products.

Speakers bio:
From Mercadona Tech, Miguel Martín Tapia and Rubén Ibáñez Pinillo will share their experience building and leading data projects at one of Spain’s most innovative retail companies.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-\ channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Utopicus Paseo de la Habana

Paseo de la Habana 9,11 (Entrada coworking Utopicus) · Madrid

Madrid dbt Meetup #6 (in-person)

Hosted by Shay B. and Alex De La C.

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, DataOps, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍 Venue Host: Utopicus Habana (P.º de La Habana, 9, 11, 28036 Madrid)
🍕 Catering: Drinks & Pizza at the place of the event
🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

*To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: ***https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

🗓️Agenda:

18:45 - 19:00 | Welcome
19:00 - 19:15 | Introduction
19:15 - 19:45 | Talk 1 - Redefining Data Workflows: How dbt Cloud Transformed Our Data Quality Strategy
20:00 - 20:30 | Talk 2 - Why Data Exploration is Key for Data Quality, Data Transformation, Data Visualization
20:30 - 22h00 | Networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1:
In this session, we’ll take you behind the scenes of our journey to redefine data workflows and elevate data quality by migrating to dbt Cloud. From modernizing our transformation processes to integrating data testing and automation into our pipelines, we’ll share the challenges we faced, the solutions we implemented, and the lessons we learned along the way.

Speaker bio:
Ramon Marrero is the Senior Head of Data & Machine Learning Operations at DISH Digital Solutions, a METRO AG company focused on advancing the digital transformation of the hospitality industry.

Since joining DISH, Ramon Marrero has led the Data and ML Operations team, driving the design and development of a robust data ecosystem. Under Ramon's leadership, the team has implemented advanced data processes, governance frameworks, and analytics capabilities that empower both the Data Science and Business Intelligence teams to deliver impactful insights.

---

🗣️Presentation #2:
In this presentation, Victoriano will show examples of how we built a UI (Graphext) for fast data exploration and why it is key for Data Quality, Data Transformation, and Data Visualization.

Speaker bio:
Victoriano Izquierdo is the co-founder of Graphext. Computers Scientist. 10 years in the data space creating a platform for visual analytics and working with over 200 companies on data problems.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-\ channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 8:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 6:45 PM to 10:00 PM CET

Utopicus Paseo de la Habana

Paseo de la Habana 9,11 (Entrada coworking Utopicus) · Madrid

Madrid dbt Meetup #5 (in-person)

Hosted by Alex De La C.

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, DataOps, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍 Venue Host: Utopicus Habana (P.º de La Habana, 9, 11, 28036 Madrid)
🍕 Catering: Drinks & Pizza at the place of the event
🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

*To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: ***https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

🗓️Agenda:

18:45 - 19:00 | Welcome
19:00 - 19:20 | Data validation with dbt: Going beyond testing (Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
19:25 - 19:45 | How Airflow + dbt Work Together at Okta (Miquel Angel Andreu Febrer - Okta)
19:50 - 20:10 | Leveraging column-level lineage to scale your dbt projects (Benoit Perigaud - dbt)
20:15 - 22h00 | Networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1:
dbt can be leveraged for more than just basic testing. We will dive into advanced data validation techniques that ensure data quality beyond conventional testing in dbt. We will use Recce as a new emerging tool that allows validation checks and improved approval requests.

Speaker bio: Alejandro de la Cruz López is an experienced Data Engineer with a strong background in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence. He has led various data projects, optimizing systems and improving infrastructure for several organizations. Alejandro holds multiple professional certifications and has authored articles on data engineering practices. His work focuses on delivering efficient, scalable data solutions in the cloud.

---

🗣️Presentation #2:
In this presentation, Miquel Angel will show how Okta dynamically builds all dbt DAGs from upstream to downstream based on tags and the dbt project structure, automates tests inside the dags, and uses the same warehouse configuration for both dbt runs and tests.

Speaker bio: Data Engineer specialized in ETL, BigData processes, and DevOps

🗣️Presentation #3:
Today, we have tools to enforce quality checks on projects, at the model level, like dbt_project_evaluator. Those tools are indispensable to allow teams to scale their dbt transformation. But while we've been focusing on rules at the model level. Could we leverage CLL to also define rules at the column level now? The idea of this talk would be to build an open-source tool and present what problems it can solve.

Speaker bio: Staff Analytics Engineer at dbt Labs

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-\ channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, DataOps, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍 Venue Host: Utopicus Habana (P.º de La Habana, 9, 11, 28036 Madrid)
🍕 Catering: Drinks & Pizza at the place of the event
🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

*To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: ***https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy
🗓️Agenda:

19h00: welcome
19h05: dbt semantic layer (Charles Verleyen & Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
19h35: break
19h45: Standardizing pipelines with DBT without dying trying (Ruben Ibañez Pinillo & Miguel Martin Tapia - Mercadona Tech)
20h15: networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1: We will explore the concepts of a semantic layer and why it has been gaining traction recently. We will talk about why it is a foundation pillar for LLM and finish with a hands-on demo.
Speakers bio:
Charles is a data architect/engineer and has been working for the last 10 years in different industries before founding his company recently with a focus on providing modern data services.
Alejandro is a Data architect passionate about data, automation, and AI. He has recently shifted left on the data engineering aspect of data and is a savvy dbt engineer.
---
🗣️Presentation #2: The talk explores the benefits and challenges of using DBT, a powerful tool for building and managing data transformations, to achieve standardization without sacrificing sanity. We will see best practices for leveraging DBT's features to streamline data pipelines, improve data quality, and achieve consistency across an organization's data infrastructure.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

Madrid dbt Meetup #3 (in-person)

Hosted by Charles V. and 2 others

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍 Venue Host: Utopicus Habana (P.º de La Habana, 9, 11, 28036 Madrid)

🍕 Catering: Drinks & Pizza at the place of the event

🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

🗓️Agenda:

18h45: welcome
19h00: Multi-projects collaboration with dbt Cloud (Charles Verleyen & Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
19h30: break
19h40: Behold the “plugin” - Reusing dbt pipelines and standardising data access across multiple clients (Javier Asensio & Juan Alonso - Octopus Energy)
20h10: networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1: dbt cloud is gaining a lot of traction due to its accessibility and important new data mesh features that facilitate multi projects collaboration. In this talk, we will cover the basics of dbt cloud and the multi-projects collaboration features (dependencies.yml, model versioning, data contracts, etc.). All this wiil be demoed with the "jaffle shop" repositories.

Speakers bio:

Charles is a data architect/engineer and has been working for the last 10 years in different industries before founding his company recently with a focus on providing modern data services.

Alejandro is a Data architect passionate about data, automation, and AI. He has recently shifted left on the data engineering aspect of data and is a savvy dbt engineer.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: Octopus energy provides energy in 8 different countries. Every single provider is a wee bit the same but a wee bit different. dbt groups based plugins allow us to reuse the common parts, tailor for the specifics, and automatically standardise how data is used.

Speaker bio:

Javier Asensio is the head of data platform engineering at Kraken technologies. After spending some time jumping from startup to startup for a while, he has been moving data from one place to another for five years for the Octopus Energy Group.

Juan Alonso has changed his career path from civil engineering to data science. He has worked in ArcerlorMittal and enjoys playing with energy data in Octopus Energy since 2022.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Thu, Oct 5, 2023 · 6:45 PM to 9:45 PM CEST

Utopicus

C. del Príncipe de Vergara, 112 · Madrid, MD

Madrid dbt Meetup #2 (in-person)

Hosted by Charles V. and 2 others

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you’ll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

📍Venue Host: Utopicus Principe de Vergara (C. del Príncipe de Vergara, 112, 28002 Madrid)

🍕 Catering: Beers & Pizza at the place of the event

🤝 Organizer: Astrafy is organizing this event, enabled by the community team at dbt Labs

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

🗓️Agenda:

18h45: welcome
19h00: dbt history and its potential for data quality (Charles Verleyen & Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
19h30: break
19h40: Managing alerting workflow with dbt (Mikkel Dengsoe - Synq)
20h10: networking with drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

🗣️Presentation #1: dbt history and its potential for data qualityt

Description: from its genesis in 2016 until its massive fundraising in recent years, dbt has revolutionized the data world in just a few years. In this talk we will look back at the key factors that made dbt so popular and we will then talk about how dbt can help with data quality.

Speakers bio:

Charles is a data architect/engineer and has been working for the last 10 years in different industries before founding his company recently with a focus on providing modern data services.

Alejandro is a Data architect passionate about data, automation, and AI. He has recently shifted left on the data engineering aspect of data and is a savvy dbt engineer.

---

🗣️Presentation #2: Managing alerting workflow with dbt

Description: Managing the alerting workflow is no easy task as you scale. On one side you want to know about issues as they happen but at the same time you don’t want to end up flooded with data alerts that don’t get actioned. When data teams don’t find the right balance and end up having too many alerts that are unaddressed it can lead to negative consequences such as

Important issues get overlooked
Triaging and monitoring alerts become a time sink
The data team loses confidence in the impact of writing tests

In this talk Mikkel will dive into what a better alerting flow looks like and give go through how you can get use metadata from the dbt manifest.json to bring in ownership, root cause and importance to your alerts.

Speaker bio:

Mikkel is the co-founder of Synq. Prior to Synq, he was leading data teams at the UK challenger bank Monzo seeing the team scale to 100 people and thousands of data models. He frequently writes about all things data at https://mikkeldengsoe.substack.com/

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-madrid channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Thu, Jun 29, 2023 · 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM CEST

P.º de La Habana, 9, 11

P.º de La Habana, 9 · Madrid, MD

Madrid dbt Meetup #1 (in-person)

Hosted by Charles V. and Alex De La C.

Madrid dbt Meetup
5.0
22 reviews
Details

The 1st edition of the Madrid dbt Meetup will take place in Utopicus (paseo de la habana) on June 29th. This group is dedicated to bringing together professionals and enthusiasts interested in exploring the latest developments and trends in the field of analytics engineering. Whether you're a seasoned veteran or just starting out, we encourage you to come along and join the conversation.

Schedule
18h30: welcome with food & drinks
19h00: Data Mesh with dbt (Charles Verleyen & Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
19h45: break
20h00: Real-time analytics with Google Cloud Asset inventory data and dbt (Alejandro Martín Valledor - Tinybird)
20h30: networking & drinks & pizzas 🍺 🍕

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Be part of the conversation in the channel #local-madrid in dbt Slack!

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Data Mesh with dbt (Charles Verleyen & Alejandro de la Cruz Lopez - Astrafy)
Data Mesh has become a powerful buzzwords and every data practitioner has heard about it. The major problem with buzzwords is that there are a lot of interpretations around them and this can generate quite some frustration. In case of data mesh, it can also slow down the adoption of this amazing new paradigm.
In this session I will demystify the main concepts of data mesh and bring those to life with dbt with concrete data products from Astrafy real data. You can expect to see data mesh in actions in a very pragmatic way as we will cover as well activation of data from those data products by downstream applications (leveraging dbt semantic layer).

Charles is a data architect/engineer and has been working for the last 10 years in different industries before founding his company recently with a focus on providing modern data services.

Alejandro is a MLOps engineers passionate about data in general. He is recently shift left on the data engineering aspect of data and is a savvy dbt engineer.

Real-time analytics with Google Cloud Asset inventory data and dbt (Alejandro Martín Valledor - Tinybird)
The future of data is real-time. Tinybird has become a key player in the real-time analytics world and they are shaping the future of this new paradigm of analytics. In this session, they will demo how real-time analytics and datamart tables built from dbt can seamlessly work together to produce great insights.
